observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and  management  agreed to comply with the following:


Tighten the internal control over the grant of financial assistance such as inclusion of the Municipal Social Welfare Development Officer on the proper screening of the grantees giving priority to the less privileged constituents and assistance be extended only once to the same persons/organizations, neighborhood associations.

The Municipal Accountant-ICO and the Municipal Treasurer to check on the completeness of supporting documents and see to it that the LCE made a marginal note on the face of the clean and pre-numbered Barangay Certification indicating the amount to be granted and his signature to appear in the face of the Disbursement Voucher for propriety and legality of the transaction and for approval. 

Further, the Municipal Accountant-ICO assure the authenticity of the signatures of the concerned Punong Barangays in the face of the attached barangay certification to provide an internal control over the grant of financial assistance.
observations.managementAction: The LGU offered no explanation/ justification or action taken on this particular finding.  The audit recommendation remained unimplemented  at year-end. Instead, management only made assurance to check the completeness of supporting documents and assured the authenticity of the PB signatures on the certifications attached to the disbursement vouchers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the General Services Office, and the Engineering Office to strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 76-34, 87-278 and 2009-001 on the submission of purchase orders and contracts.
observations.managementAction: The audit recommendation remained unimplemented at year-end. Purchase orders and contracts were not submitted to COA Office within five (5) days after perfection thereof. RDs and RCIs with corresponding paid vouchers are not, likewise regularly submitted to COA Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly implement the withholding of ten percent (10%) retention money on all payments of infrastructure projects to cover for defects that may arise before final acceptance and assure all the necessary documents are complete before processing claims on infrastructure projects.  Likewise, we recommended that the BAC Chairman document properly all the activities to be undertaken.  Attach attendance sheets to validate the occurrence of the all procurement activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Social Worker  submit the required monitoring reports and assure that every liquidation report has complete documentation as enumerated in the preceding paragraphs, in compliance with Administrative Order No. 08, series of 2012 and the Omnibus Guidelines in the implementation of the supplemental feeding program and the provisions of COA Circular No. 94-013.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE)  require the Municipal Accountant to strictly follow the maximum rate allowed for advance payment which is fifteen percent (15%) and process the same only upon submission to and acceptance by the procuring entity of an irrevocable standby letter of credit, bank guarantee or a surety bond as prescribed by Item 4.2 of the Annex E of 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 CY 2016. We also recommended that the Accountant require the submission of all  the  documentary requirements  consistent with Section 9.1.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct his Municipal Accountant not to process claims of progress billings payment unless all the required supporting documents are present and attached to the disbursement vouchers and comply strictly with Section 9.1.1.1 to 9.1.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly comply with the reporting requirement on the fund utilization pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 in line with the LDRRM Investment Plan.  We recommended, in particular, that the Municipal Accountant  prepare the required monthly report and the same shall be submitted to this Office by the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office (LDRRMO) on or before 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Disaster Council (LDC).

We recommended that management require the concerned officials to prepare and submit the Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan, subject for review of the National Solid Waste Management Commission and comply the rules of Republic Act No. 9003 in order to benefit the constituents of the Municipality.

We recommended and management agreed to strictly comply with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 especially on:
GAD Plan and Budget of five percent (5%) of the total annual appropriations.
Assure that GAD Accomplishments per PPAs are in line with the GAD Plan.
Reported accomplishments should be duly supported with (a) brief summary of the reported program or project; (b) copies of reported policy issuances; (c) results of Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) test results.

We recommended that the management submit all contracts together with the required supporting documents to COA  to facilitate review, evaluation and inspection in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Update the preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements and effect the adjustments accordingly on all reconciling items.
Demand from the Municipal Treasurer to submit the corresponding vouchers for unrecorded withdrawals/disbursements totaling P103,030.01 under Trust Fund.
Restore the cash and payable accounts on stale checks totaling totaling P19,261.16.
Demand deposit slips from the Municipal Treasurer regarding the P38,300.00 in transit reconciling item and coordinate with the bank for that matter.
Explain the non-recording of adjustments of long outstanding reconciling items particularly that of the unrecorded disbursements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the DA-RFU2 regarding the status of the loan account  under the “loan at cost”. Make proper representation with DA-RFO2 for possible write-off and  dropping of the said property from the books of the LGU in case the property is already unserviceable.  Otherwise, follow the provisions cited in the Memorandum of Agreement and require him to prepare the Journal Entry Voucher to recognize Loans Payable- Due to DA-RFU RO2 and the booking-up of the property.
Further, require the Municipal Treasurer to provide record to show that the P2,000,000.00 was recorded or not in the Cashbook.

We recommended that management comply with the documentary requirements under RA No. 9502 and EO No. 49 in all procurement of drugs and medicines and ensure that the practice of paying purchases through reimbursements be stop. Instead, issue checks payable directly to the supplier.

We  recommended and  management  agreed to comply with the following;
Tighten the internal control over the grant of financial assistance such as inclusion of the Municipal Social Welfare Development Officer on the proper screening of the grantees giving priority to the less privileged constituents and assistance be extended only once to the same persons/organizations, neighborhood associations.
The Municipal Accountant-ICO and the Municipal Treasurer to check on the completeness of supporting documents and see to it that the LCE made a marginal note on the face of the clean and pre-numbered Barangay Certifications indicating the amount to be granted and his signature to appear in the face of the Disbursement Voucher for propriety and legality of the transaction and for approval.
Further, the Municipal Accountant-ICO assure the authenticity of the signatures of the concerned Punong Barangays in the face of the attached Barangay Certification to provide an internal control over the grant of financial assistance.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and other persons concerned  submit immediately for audit the Municipality’s accounts and all financial records mentioned earlier.  Henceforth, he LGU is required to comply within the deadline set under Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 100 of PD 1445 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations in the submission of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Treasurer, Inventory Committee, Property Officer, Municipal Engineer and all other officer/employees concerned to submit the required reports regularly and adhere strictly the provisions cited in the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I and II, COA Circular Nos. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 and 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
inform the Audit Team within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the project/activity that the appropriate signboards and/or public notice are already posted.
Nonetheless, failure on the part of the officials concerned to submit the documents and reports mentioned herein shall automatically cause the suspension of the official/employee concerned. Further, no appropriation authorized in the General Appropriations Act shall be available to pay the salary or any official or employee who violates the provisions of this section, without prejudice to any disciplinary action that may be instituted against such official or employee. All of these were provided in Section 122(2 and 3) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We recommended that management require the General Services Office, and the Engineering Office to strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 76-34, 87-278 and 2009-001 on the submission of purchase orders and contracts, otherwise transactions will be suspended in audit as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We  recommended and management agreed to:

Submit the required documents needed for audit review and evaluation on time.

Strictly implement within the time frame all planned programs/projects identified during the budget year to meet the goals set and provide effective services to the public.

Maintain a special account and a complete set of subsidiary ledgers to enable the generation of financial statements at any given time.

Submit complete supporting documents on the disbursements on time, as required/listed under COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, if any.

We further recommended that the management enforce strictly the provisions cited in Section 287 and 324 of Local Government Code of the Philippines and Section 4.4 of Joint Circular No. 2004-01.
observations.managementAction: The only recommendation that was not implemented by management is the maintenance of a special account and complete set of subsidiary ledgers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management  request the Sangguniang Bayan to pass a resolution authorizing the Municipal Treasurer to open a separate depository account where the unutilized balance of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund shall be transferred in accordance to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We further recommended that management complete the data needed for unexpended/unobligated balances for CY2010-2015 to determine the total unexpended balances due for deposit immediately to special trust fund account and ensure that all disbursements are within the limits set in the LDRRMD Investment Plan as guided by RA 10121.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer opened a Special Trust Fund account with the bank for the LDRRM Fund thru SB Resolution No. 16, s-2015, in compliance with the mandate of RA No. 10121 on the utilization of LDRRM Fund.

The LGU have completed the data needed for unexpended/unobligated balances for CY 2010-2015.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to require   the Local School Board to prepare a more representative and effective annual budget for the Special Education Fund in order to utilize available funds in productive activities and projects to achieve higher standards of education and better schools for the youth conducive to social development and advancement.  Effective budgeting, fast and timely implementation of programs/projects are necessary to maximize the use of the available funds.
observations.managementAction: The LGU made close coordination with the Local School Board in the planning and implementation of projects and activities to maximize available resources allotted to Special Education Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management observe  that the 20 % of the Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) be optimally programed and utilized strictly in accordance with the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011, issued by the Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG) and the Department of Budget and Management (DBM) and Section 287 of the Local Government Code of the Philippines, Book II to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.
observations.managementAction: Management have  maximized the use of the 20% Economic Development Funds and other trust funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant - ICO agreed to prioritize the preparation and submission of the Bank Reconciliation Statements on a monthly basis to establish the correct balances of the Cash in Bank accounts for all funds as required in Section 74 of Presidential Decree 1445 and Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.  Should there be any adjustments, the same shall be effected immediately to avoid accumulation thereof and to present the correct balance of the cash in bank accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Acting Municipal Accountant   prepared and submitted to COA Office Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) for all funds as of July 12, 2016. The BRS for the remaining months of CY2016 is still in process of preparation.
Out of Php234,349.62 unliquidated cash advances, the amount of Php91,720.00 was settled leaving a balance of Php142,629.62 as of December 31, 2016.
observations.reason: Findings on the balance of unliquidated cash advances with the corresponding recommendation was restated under Part II.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation and management agreed to: 

Stop the practice of withdrawing cash for payment of municipal obligations and strictly implement the issuance of checks to pay for all its obligations.  In cases where it is difficult, impractical or impossible to use checks in settling obligations, cash payments shall be made through a cash advance by a duly designated disbursing officer or the agency may opt to establish a Petty Cash Fund for its petty expenses in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.  The management should strictly implement the prescribed controls over cash transactions to ensure its proper accounting.

Assure that the payments of payroll fund be duly signed/approved by all of the concerned officials and ensure complete documentation.
observations.managementAction: Withdrawals for payment of salaries and wages is now based on the net of payroll prepared for the period. Petty cash fund was set-up which will be used for payment of petty expenses. As per COA Circular No. 91-350 dated March 4, 1990, ceiling on cash payment was raised from Php5,000 to Php10,000 in each case in view of the recent increased in price of basic commodities so as not to hamper the day-to-day operations of the government, except for burial and medical assistance, considering the urgency of need, and  in the name of the payee. There is no appointed DO and Cashier.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management agreed to create an inventory committee to conduct complete physical inventory of the Municipality’s property, plant and equipment and submit a report thereon to the Office of the Auditor, in accordance with Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS, Volume 1.  

Further, we recommended and the Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer and the Accountant agreed to maintain and update their property cards and equipment ledger cards, respectively, to facilitate the inventory taking and reconciliation between the property and accounting records.
observations.managementAction: The LGU created an Inventory Committee supposedly to conduct a complete physical inventory of all the municipality’s plant, property and equipment, however no inventory has been conducted yet at year-end. Property cards and equipment ledger cards were not maintained and updated, resulting to non-reconciliation between property and accounting records.
observations.reason: Portion of the unimplemented recommendation with the corresponding finding was restated in Part II.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to strengthen their tax collection campaign and adopt measures and strategies to improve collection efficiency and apply coordinated efforts in the implementation of real property tax program in the Municipality and on the barangay levels to maximize collection of taxes. We further recommend that, if possible, formulate policies to standardize the giving of tax incentives or relief to delinquent taxpayers as well.  Otherwise, enforce the above cited civil remedies as stated in Chapter IV, Title I, Book II of R.A. 7160.
observations.managementAction: Tax campaign has been conducted by the LGU to the different barangays of the municipality, but because of drought and typhoons, farmers which represent 80% of RPT delinquencies failed to pay. Nevertheless, lists of delinquent taxpayers have been submitted to the Provincial Government of Isabela for the initiation of civil remedies since RPT is a provincial imposition. The LGU have  no existing local incentive and investment code as basis in giving incentives.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to ensure that job order contracts issued to all job order employees are in accordance with the pro-forma of job order contract and contain the necessary stipulations as required under CSC Memorandum Circular No. 24, s. 2002, as amended.  This would ensure that the hiring of job orders is properly justified and actually needed by the municipal government.  The contracts should also be signed by the hired job order employee and duly notarized by proper authorities.

We also recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to   submit all the required documents before processing and payment of the claims for wages/salaries of the job order employees.
observations.managementAction: Management have complied with the requirements of CSC Memorandum Circular No. 24,s. 2002.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit comments and/or justifications from the personnel concerned (such as, BAC members who undertook the procurement), as well as those who prepared the contracts – Purchase Orders for the abovementioned items, as to their bases of resorting to alternative mode of procurement which resulted to the issuance of several Purchase Orders and justify the reported disbursements as against  Report of Checks Issued by providing the Audit Team  a Report of Utilization and Disbursements detailing the check date, check number, payee and amount of disbursements made.
observations.managementAction: Management have submitted their explanations/justifications on the said findings and were found to be meritorious.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ensure the approval of the Local Chief Executive or his duly authorized representative and signatures of responsible officials and employees on the various claims before effecting payments.
observations.managementAction: Management have implemented the recommendation beginning in CY2016 transactions.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant-ICO use the Perpetual Inventory Method in booking all purchases of drugs and medicines/office supplies/accountable forms as required in the NGAS Manual to correctly present the expense and inventory accounts in the financial statements.

We also recommended that the management adopt measures and procedures in the procurement, distribution, recording and monitoring of drugs and medicines, office supplies, accountable forms, in order to safeguard government resources and promote efficiency and economy in its operations.
observations.managementAction: Management have implemented the recommendation beginning in CY 2016 transactions. Perpetual Inventory Method was used in booking up all purchases of drugs and medicines and supplies.
